intro: Cohere and Apidog bill differently, which matters more than feature parity here — Cohere sells a subscription from $4.00 with a free tier, while Apidog offers a free tier, paid from $672/YEAR. Cohere is LLM API focused on enterprise text, embeddings, and RAG. Apidog is all-in-one API design, mocking, testing, and documentation tool. Pick Cohere when the job is api gateway and rate limiting and you accept medium lock-in; pick Apidog when it is api gateway and rate limiting and you accept medium lock-in. The data we have shows Cohere at mostly transparent pricing and docs with a polished developer experience, and Apidog at mostly transparent pricing and docs with a polished developer experience. The honest trade-off: neither is universal — Cohere is a poor fit for internal, simple projects, and Apidog is a poor fit for internal, simple projects. Match the pricing model and lock-in level to how your team actually works, not the feature list.
quick_take: Cohere is for LLM API focused on enterprise text; Apidog is for all-in-one API design; decide based on pricing model fit.
verdict_a: Choose Cohere if a subscription at $4.00 and you want an entry-level free tier fits the budget and the work maps to LLM API focused on enterprise text, embeddings, and RAG.
verdict_b: Choose Apidog if day-to-day developer ergonomics are a priority and the work lines up with all-in-one API design, mocking, testing, and documentation tool.

faq.a: Cohere starts at $4.00 on a subscription model; Apidog starts at $672/YEAR on a freemium model. That makes the sticker comparison depend on usage — a low entry price can get expensive once volume ramps, so price at your actual workload.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Cohere to Apidog?
faq.a: Migration is possible in either direction. Cohere carries medium lock-in and Apidog carries medium lock-in, so plan for meaningful but not blocking rework. Run both in parallel before you fully cut over.

Both Cohere and Apidog rate the same on developer experience (5/5). The decision on DX then comes down to taste — which CLI, UI, or workflow matches your team's habits. A short side-by-side trial is the quickest way to tell.

faq.q: Is Apidog a good alternative to Cohere?
faq.a: Yes — Apidog is a reasonable alternative to Cohere for api gateway and rate limiting. The practical differences are subscription-vs-freemium billing and medium-vs-medium lock-in. If those fit your constraints better, treat Apidog as a credible swap.
